hero.sub: If you can chat, have taste, and know what you need — you can get anything.

items.line: Done — your signup form is ready. Tap any field to edit.
Dashboard's ready — swap metrics and charts anytime.

items.q: Set up a leave approval flow
items.line: Flow's wired up — reorder steps however you like.
Survey's generated — add or edit questions freely.

works.label: Works
works.title: Recent work
works.subtitle: Each one solves a real problem in a real scenario.

items.name: Form Design
items.desc: Say a sentence, get an online form that collects data. Edit fields by chatting; share a link or QR to fill; responses auto-aggregate.

items.name: Next up
items.desc: Same approach, a different thing. Still polishing — forms first. Curious? Stay tuned.

items.name: Your idea
items.desc: Already have something in mind? Tell me what you need and I'll build it. Starts with one sentence.

items.title: What kind of projects do you take?
items.content: Chat-driven small tools, agents, and internal workflow tools fit best — the kind you can describe in a sentence and operate by conversation. I can't take on huge systems solo, but I can help you think through whether and how to build.

items.title: How do you charge?
items.content: Depends on scope. Small tools might be a friend price or done on the side; serious custom work is quoted per project. Model calls usually go through BYOK (your own key), so that cost never passes through me.

items.title: How long does it take?
items.content: I work solo — good things take time. Simple ones: a few days; complex ones are scheduled by plan. I'd rather give an honest timeline than promise one I can't meet.

items.title: I just have an idea, not fully thought out — can I still reach out?
items.content: Of course. Talking a fuzzy idea into clarity is the first step anyway. Even if the conclusion is 'not worth building,' that saved you time.

items.title: What's next for Chat × Everything?
items.content: Forms are the first stop. Next I'll make more small things the same way. What exactly? A little secret — finish one, then talk about the next.
